phase1.explanation:
JavaFX is the modern GUI framework for Java desktop applications.

--- JavaFX vs Swing ---

Swing (old):
• Older, still supported
• Heavyweight components
• More complex event handling
• Not hardware accelerated

JavaFX (modern):
• Newer, actively developed
• Lightweight, hardware accelerated
• CSS styling support
• FXML for UI design
• Built-in animation
• Better for modern UIs

--- Key Concepts ---

Application Class:
• Entry point for JavaFX applications
• Extend Application and override start()
• launch() method starts the application

Stage:
• The main window/top-level container
• Each application has a primary Stage
• Can create additional stages (windows)

Scene:
• Container for all UI components
• Holds the scene graph
• Can be switched (navigation)

Scene Graph:
• Tree of nodes (UI elements)
• Parent nodes contain child nodes
• Root node is the top of the scene graph

--- JavaFX Thread ---

JavaFX has its own thread:
• Platform.runLater() — schedule code on JavaFX thread
• All UI updates MUST happen on JavaFX thread
• Background tasks should use separate threads

--- Setting Up JavaFX ---

Requirements:
• Java 8+ (Java 11+ requires separate JavaFX SDK)
• JavaFX SDK or Maven/Gradle dependencies
• For Java 11+: add --module-path and --add-modules

Maven dependency:
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.openjfx</groupId>
    <artifactId>javafx-controls</artifactId>
    <version>17.0.2</version>
</dependency>

--- Common JavaFX Components ---

Controls:
• Button, Label, TextField, TextArea
• CheckBox, RadioButton, ComboBox
• ListView, TableView, TreeView

Layouts:
• VBox, HBox, GridPane, BorderPane
• StackPane, FlowPane, AnchorPane

Properties and Bindings:
• Observable properties (StringProperty, IntegerProperty)
• Bindings for automatic updates

FXML:
• XML-based UI design
• Separate UI from logic
• Scene Builder for visual design

CSS Styling:
• CSS-like syntax for styling
• -fx- properties (JavaFX specific)
• Can use external stylesheets

Animation:
• Timeline, KeyFrame, KeyValue
• Transitions (Translate, Fade, Rotate)

JavaFX is the future of Java desktop development!

phase2.explanation:
Conceptual lesson — no broken code!

Common misconception: "JavaFX is dead and Swing is the only option."

Actually: JavaFX is actively maintained and is the recommended choice for new desktop applications. Oracle still supports JavaFX, and the open-source community continues to develop it.

Another misconception: "JavaFX is only for simple UIs."

Actually: JavaFX can create complex, professional UIs with:
• Charts and graphs
• 3D graphics
• Web views (embedded browser)
• Media playback (audio/video)
• Complex animations

You can build full-featured applications with JavaFX!

phase3.prompt:
Which statement about JavaFX is TRUE?

A) JavaFX doesn't support CSS styling
B) All UI updates must happen on the JavaFX Application Thread
C) JavaFX applications don't use a main() method
D) JavaFX is older than Swing

Select the CORRECT statement.
dialogueHints: Think about how JavaFX handles UI updates...
CSS styling is a JavaFX feature.
Which framework came first, Swing or JavaFX?
phase3.openingDialogue: JavaFX — modern GUI for modern apps! 🎨
